items.title: Cloud credentials directory created without mode 0700 — API tokens world-listable on multi-user systems
items.intent: The cloud credentials directory must not be listable by other users — it stores API tokens and must be created with mode 0700 so directory contents are not exposed, matching the protection applied to credentials.ts file itself.
items.description: src/cloud/provisioning/credentials.ts line 53 calls mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) without a mode option when creating the cloud/ directory. This creates the directory with default permissions (0755), making it world-readable. While credentials.json is written with mode 0600 (correct), any user on the system can ls the cloud/ directory to enumerate that cloud credentials exist. Same pattern: registry.ts line 57 and snapshot-registry.ts line 51 both call mkdirSync without mode. registry.ts is already covered by BUG-077. snapshot-registry.ts stores non-sensitive data (provider, region, version, timestamp) but should be consistent. Fix: pass { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 } to all three mkdirSync calls.
steps: Read src/cloud/provisioning/credentials.ts lines 50-55 — confirm mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) has no mode option
Change mkdirSync in credentials.ts to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 })
Read src/cloud/provisioning/snapshot-registry.ts lines 48-53 — confirm mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) has no mode option
Change mkdirSync in snapshot-registry.ts to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 })
Run credentials tests and snapshot-registry tests — confirm both pass with no regressions
acceptanceCriteria: The cloud/ directory (deployDir/cloud/) is created with mode 0700 — stat confirms no group/other read bits
snapshot-registry parent directory is created with mode 0700
File permissions on credentials.json and snapshots.json remain unchanged at 0600
items.groomingNotes: RE-GROOMED 2026-03-23 (PO pass). Priority elevated should→must for consistency with BUG-081 (Integration credentials dir, same pattern, shipped as must). Cloud credentials directory stores API tokens — world-enumerable is a must-fix security exposure, not discretionary. All tech lead grooming notes preserved; no step changes.

items.title: Restore temp directory created in /tmp without mode 0700 — decrypted backup contents world-readable during restore
items.intent: Decrypted backup contents MUST NOT be readable by other users during restore — the temp directory in /tmp must be created with mode 0700 before any decrypted data lands in it.
items.description: src/operate/backup/restore.ts line 151 calls mkdirSync(tempDir, { recursive: true }) where tempDir = join(tmpdir(), 'clawhq-restore-{timestamp}'). This creates the temp directory in /tmp with default permissions (0755), making it world-readable. The restore pipeline then decrypts the encrypted backup archive INTO this temp directory (line 174: decryptedPath = join(tempDir, 'snapshot.tar.gz')). During the decrypt and extract phases, the decrypted backup contents (including credentials.json, .env with API tokens, agent config, memory data) are temporarily readable by any user on the system. Fix: import DIR_MODE_SECRET from config/defaults.js and pass mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET to mkdirSync at line 151. Also apply the same fix to extractDir at line 196 which holds the extracted files.
steps: Read src/operate/backup/restore.ts lines 14-22 — confirm mkdirSync is imported but DIR_MODE_SECRET is not
Add DIR_MODE_SECRET to the existing import from ../../config/defaults.js at line 21
Line 151: change mkdirSync(tempDir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(tempDir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET })
Line 196: change mkdirSync(extractDir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(extractDir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET })
Create src/operate/backup/restore.test.ts — use vi.spyOn(fs, 'mkdirSync') to capture mode argument; verify first mkdirSync call (tempDir creation) passes mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET (0o700)
Run npm test -- --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | tail -20 to confirm all tests pass
acceptanceCriteria: restore.ts line 151 (tempDir) creates the temp directory with mode DIR_MODE_SECRET — stat confirms no group/other read bits
restore.ts line 196 (extractDir) creates the extract directory with mode DIR_MODE_SECRET
DIR_MODE_SECRET is imported from config/defaults.js in restore.ts
restore.test.ts exists and the mkdirSync mode test passes
items.groomingNotes: GROOMED 2026-03-23. Source verified at specified lines. Two mkdirSync calls in restore.ts need mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET — tempDir (line 151) and extractDir (line 196). DIR_MODE_SECRET not currently imported in restore.ts. Test approach: spy on mkdirSync to capture mode arg since a real restore requires GPG + real archives. Exports: restoreSnapshot is exported. Priority must is correct — decrypted backup contents (credentials, .env, config) land in /tmp world-readable during restore window.

items.title: Backup snapshots directory created without mode 0700 — backup archives world-listable
items.intent: The backup snapshots directory MUST be created with mode 0700 so that backup archive existence and timestamps cannot be enumerated by other system users.
items.description: src/operate/backup/backup.ts line 166 calls mkdirSync(snapsDir, { recursive: true }) without a mode option when creating ops/backup/snapshots/. This creates the snapshots directory with default permissions (0755), making it world-listable. Any user on the system can ls the snapshots directory to see that backups exist and read their timestamps and snapshot IDs. While the backup archives are GPG-encrypted, their existence and metadata should not be exposed. Fix: import DIR_MODE_SECRET from config/defaults.js (currently not imported — only BACKUP_GPG_TIMEOUT_MS is imported from defaults.js) and change mkdirSync(snapsDir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(snapsDir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET }). No chmodSync needed — snapshotsDir is always fresh-created at this call site.
steps: Read src/operate/backup/backup.ts lines 16-26 — confirm DIR_MODE_SECRET is not imported from defaults.js
Read src/operate/backup/backup.ts lines 155-170 — confirm mkdirSync(snapsDir, { recursive: true }) has no mode option
Add DIR_MODE_SECRET to the existing import from ../../config/defaults.js (currently only BACKUP_GPG_TIMEOUT_MS is imported)
Change mkdirSync(snapsDir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(snapsDir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET })
Check if src/operate/backup/backup.test.ts exists — if so, add a test that stats the snapshots directory and asserts mode & 0o777 === 0o700 after calling the backup function
Run npm test -- --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | tail -20 to confirm all tests pass
acceptanceCriteria: The ops/backup/snapshots/ directory is created with mode 0700 — stat confirms no group/other read bits
DIR_MODE_SECRET is added to the import from config/defaults.js in backup.ts
All existing backup tests continue to pass
items.groomingNotes: GROOMED 2026-03-23. Source verified: backup.ts line 166 confirmed mkdirSync(snapsDir, { recursive: true }) without mode. DIR_MODE_SECRET not currently imported in backup.ts. Single mkdirSync call to fix. No chmodSync needed — snapsDir is always newly created at this call site (createSnapshot creates a fresh snapshot ID each time). Check for existing backup.test.ts before writing new tests.

items.title: approval-queue.json written without mode 0600 — pending agent action submissions world-readable
items.intent: approval-queue.json MUST be written with mode 0600 — pending action submissions can contain sensitive content (email text, message content, transaction details) and must not be world-readable.
items.description: Two code paths write approval-queue.json without a file mode, leaving it world-readable (default 0644). CODE PATH 1: src/evolve/approval/queue.ts line 59 calls 'await writeFile(queuePath(deployDir), JSON.stringify(queue, null, 2))' with no mode option. CODE PATH 2: src/design/tools/approve-action.ts (the generated tool template) line 62 calls 'writeFileSync(QUEUE_FILE, JSON.stringify(queue, null, 2))' with no mode option — QUEUE_FILE = join(__dirname, '..', 'memory', 'approval-queue.json'). The approval queue stores high-stakes pending action submissions: email content, message text, account changes, purchase details — whatever the agent submitted for user approval. Any user on the system can read pending action submissions. Fix for CODE PATH 1: change writeFile call to writeFile(queuePath(deployDir), JSON.stringify(queue, null, 2), { mode: 0o600 }). Fix for CODE PATH 2: in the generated code template string, change writeFileSync(QUEUE_FILE, JSON.stringify(queue, null, 2)) to writeFileSync(QUEUE_FILE, JSON.stringify(queue, null, 2), { mode: 0o600 }). No import needed for either fix — 0o600 literal is sufficient.
steps: Read src/evolve/approval/queue.ts lines 53-60 — confirm writeFile call has no mode option

Change 'await writeFile(queuePath(deployDir), JSON.stringify(queue, null, 2))' to 'await writeFile(queuePath(deployDir), JSON.stringify(queue, null, 2), { mode: 0o600 })' — no import needed, 0o600 literal is sufficient
Read src/design/tools/approve-action.ts lines 58-67 — confirm writeFileSync in generated template has no mode option

In the generated code template string, change 'writeFileSync(QUEUE_FILE, JSON.stringify(queue, null, 2))' to 'writeFileSync(QUEUE_FILE, JSON.stringify(queue, null, 2), { mode: 0o600 })' — no import needed, 0o600 literal in generated CJS code
Run npm test -- --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | tail -20 to confirm all tests pass
acceptanceCriteria: src/evolve/approval/queue.ts writeFile call includes { mode: 0o600 } option
src/design/tools/approve-action.ts generated template writeFileSync call includes { mode: 0o600 } option
approval-queue.json is written with mode 0600 — stat confirms no group/other read bits
items.groomingNotes: RE-GROOMED 2026-03-23 (PO pass). Priority elevated should→must for consistency with BUG-086 (commands.json without mode 0600, must) and BUG-074 (instance registry without mode 0600, must). approval-queue.json holds pending user action submissions — email content, message text, purchase details, account changes. File contents world-readable is a must-fix, not discretionary. Tech lead grooming notes preserved; no step changes.
